reviews.review_description: Some audio issues early on , but levels out. Worth 10 minutes
One of the best vr experience I've experienced so far.

Such a great app. Really informative commentary, tremendous sense of presence from the (teleporting) free movement around the 3d rendered / photogrammetry sites, great extra scenes triggered by collecting objects. 

I love this - high quality, free and a template for how historical / art content can be presented. And can be put on an SD card (properly - I checked!). I used a Gear VR controller and would recommend using that.

Would love more scenes, would be willing to pay, but thank you devs I will definitely be adding this to my vr demo list.

Must download.
Good picture quality and 2D/3D mixed solution. On the down side controls are not friendly and there's more to listen to than things to see.

Tour four UNESCO World Heritage Sites just like you were there (minutes travel expenses and heat stroke), through videos,  stereoscopic panoramas and, most importantly, full *3D SCANS* of the sites!

This is one of the most impressive apps for the Gear VR / Oculus Go I've seen so far. It's very polished, intuitive and fun to explore. And its performance is also excellent - no hiccups at all on my good old Note 4! Thanks and congrats to the developers and everyone else involved in this production! 

I've found a few shortcomings, though: the experience works best with the controller (navigating with the touchpad seems to restricted your movement and is very buggy); the stereoscopic images had artifacts (e.g., I've seen a "centaur" working on a site); there are no subtitles; there is no audio "scrubber"; narrations could be a little bit faster; text could (or should) have a larger font size.
By Cyark/Farbridge. Virtual tour of monuments in U.S., Peru & Thailand.

...when you in the main lobby picture stutters during headturning.

geometrie not completed/ finished f.ex. in the mountain village you can see the end of polygons from a ledge. 
even though there is no data captured, why couldn't you add geometrie or rocks by hand? kills immersion.

background images could be in higher resolution, at least for phones that are more powerful than s6.
and maybe textures on heometrie could be higher resolution. i don't mind longer loading times!

two 360 images are not in 3D. that is cheap...the one where you look at the president rock from afar makes sense because of scale. but the other from a mountain where you look down on village etc. has to be 3D for the feeling of height!

keep the good work flowing! thanks

Set the antialising settings to 2x. The issue was discussed on this Oculus developer forum. Just adding one line to one of your start methods should be enought to make you app much better. https://www.google.de/search?q=gear+vr+antialising&oq=gear+vr+antialising&aqs=chrome..69i57.10300j0j4&client=ms-android-samsung&sourceid=chrome-mobile&ie=UTF-8

I also experience frame rate losses on the S7. Please use the OVR Metric Tool to check your frame rates in real time.
Somenthing new and simple to use

Great app! You move around, exploring several historical places and listen to archeologists. All audio comments are under 1 min so you get short and meaningful info and can look around in the meantime. Want to see more!

A small but well-made selection of historical places to explore in 3D (full 6DOF on the Quest) using photogrammetry for realistic results. Much better than using hand-created models of the places or static 360 images - this is the right way to do VR tourism. Instead of being in a fixed vantage point, you can wander more-or-less freely around the site, listening to little explanatory points as you go. For a free app it cannot be faulted and absolutely deserves five stars. I'm well aware of how much work photogrammetry involves and this is nicely made.

That said, since this was designed for Go, I hope at some point it will be remade especially for the Quest. Some of the textures feel lower resolution than the Quest can probably handle, especially the background images. It would also be nice to have more background sounds for fuller immersion, and the narration is a tad on the dull side - some more history and less site description would be good. All in all, though, this is definitely the way forward.
